channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a variety of third-party tool integrations, enhancing the capabilities of AI agents for real-world applications. This development allows developers to connect their agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to build excitement for the upcoming keynotes.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, emphasizing the transition to agentic AI. The event will feature technical tracks on various topics, enhancing developer skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, a new framework succeeding TFLite, which offers faster GPU acceleration and better support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance efficiency in deploying GenAI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly session designed for developers to enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative focuses on practical applications of advanced topics like AI agents.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more effective coding collaborator.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to ease coding tasks by acting as an AI pair programmer. These tools aim to improve code comprehension and reduce manual effort.

item.title: Russia's Fancy Bear still attacking routers to boost fake sites, NCSC warns
item.description: The UK's National Cyber Security Centre warns that Russia's Fancy Bear group is targeting routers to steal sensitive information, affecting 200 organizations and 5,000 devices. This ongoing threat highlights the risks of cyber espionage.

item.title: Uber is the latest to be won over by Amazon’s AI chips
item.description: Uber is expanding its partnership with Amazon, opting to run more of its ride-sharing features on Amazon's AI chips, moving away from Oracle and Google. This shift signifies a strategic change in Uber's tech infrastructure.

item.title: Love window snapping on Linux? You should try a tiling window manager - here's why
item.description: Linux tiling window managers can enhance productivity by optimizing workspace organization. The article discusses their functionality and recommends six specific managers for users looking to improve their workflow.

item.title: Building real-time conversational podcasts with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
item.description: Amazon Nova Sonic has been used to create an automated podcast generator that enables real-time conversations between AI hosts, showcasing its streaming and content filtering capabilities.

item.title: Text-to-SQL solution powered by Amazon Bedrock
item.description: A new text-to-SQL solution using Amazon Bedrock allows users to convert natural language business questions into database queries, providing actionable insights.

item.title: Stack Overflow abandons redesign after loyalists criticize it
item.description: Stack Overflow has decided to abandon its planned redesign after backlash from loyal users, highlighting challenges in adapting to AI's influence on developer communities.

item.title: Anthropic ups compute deal with Google and Broadcom amid skyrocketing demand
item.description: Anthropic has expanded its compute agreement with Google and Broadcom in response to a significant increase in demand, with revenue reaching $30 billion.

item.title: Why I returned to Enlightenment Linux after 30 years: Testing the new Elive beta
item.description: The author shares their experience returning to Enlightenment Linux, highlighting the nostalgic desktop environment and the new Elive beta, though missing voice control features.

item.title: Artemis II snaps eclipse, Earthset shots on first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo
item.description: The Artemis II mission has captured stunning images of the Moon and Earth, marking the first crewed lunar flyby since Apollo, showcasing the beauty of deep space.

item.title: Google Maps can now write captions for your photos using AI
item.description: Google Maps is introducing AI-generated captions for user-uploaded photos, enhancing the way local knowledge is shared on the platform.

item.title: I tried Google Photos' new AI Enhance tool: How it crops, relights, and fixes your shots - sometimes
item.description: Google Photos has launched an AI Enhance tool that automatically improves photos by cropping, relighting, and fixing them, though it has some limitations.

item.title: Break, no fix: Apple and Samsung make repairs hard
item.description: A report reveals that Apple and Samsung devices are among the hardest to repair, with Motorola and Google ranking higher for repairability.

item.title: Verifying and optimizing post-quantum cryptography at Amazon
item.description: Amazon discusses the verification and optimization of post-quantum cryptography, focusing on balancing security, performance, and maintainability through automated reasoning.

item.title: Desalination plants in the Middle East are increasingly vulnerable
item.description: Desalination plants in the Middle East are facing increased vulnerability due to escalating conflicts, particularly in Iran, threatening water supply in the region. This situation highlights the fragility of essential resources amid geopolitical tensions.

item.title: How I calibrated my subwoofer placement for peak impact in awkward room setups
item.description: This article provides insights on optimizing subwoofer placement in home theaters, especially in non-ideal room setups. Proper placement can significantly enhance audio experience.

item.title: 4 days left to save close to $500 on TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 passes
item.description: TechCrunch is offering a limited-time discount on passes for Disrupt 2026, allowing attendees to save up to $482 if they register before April 10. This promotion encourages early sign-ups for the event.

item.title: Enabling agent-first process redesign
item.description: AI agents can transform business processes by learning and optimizing workflows dynamically, rather than relying on outdated methods. This requires a shift in how companies design their processes to fully leverage AI capabilities.

item.title: Asus' latest flagship laptop competes with the MacBook Air, but not how you'd think
item.description: Asus has launched a new flagship laptop that aims to compete with the MacBook Air, but its unique design and features may appeal differently to users. The effectiveness of this approach is still debated.

item.title: I tested the AirPods Max 2, Sony XM6, and Bose Ultra 2: Why Bose is my top pick
item.description: A comparison of the latest over-ear headphones from Apple, Sony, and Bose reveals that Bose stands out as the top choice for sound quality and comfort in real-world use.

item.title: LG G6 vs. Samsung S95H: I compared the best OLED TVs of 2026 and made a tough choice
item.description: A detailed comparison between the LG G6 and Samsung S95H OLED TVs showcases their premium features, helping consumers decide which offers better value for their needs.

item.title: Build AI-powered employee onboarding agents with Amazon Quick
item.description: The article outlines how to create AI-powered onboarding agents using Amazon Quick, which can streamline HR processes by automating tasks and improving new-hire experiences.

item.title: applyllm 0.0.9
item.description: The release of applyllm 0.0.9 introduces a Python package designed for utilizing open-source LLMs in local CUDA environments, facilitating easier access to advanced AI models.

item.title: Accelerate agentic tool calling with serverless model customization in Amazon SageMaker AI
item.description: This article discusses the customization of serverless models in Amazon SageMaker AI for improved agentic tool calling, detailing the training process and evaluation methods used.

item.title: chalk-analytics 0.1.1
item.description: Chalk-analytics 0.1.1 provides team analytics for Claude Code usage, enhancing insights into how teams utilize this AI coding tool.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.28.1.dev1
item.description: Ouroboros-ai 0.28.1.dev1 introduces a specification-first workflow engine for AI coding agents, compatible with Claude Code and Codex CLI.

item.title: OpenAI Urges New Economic Rules for the AI Era
item.description: OpenAI's report emphasizes the need for new economic policies to address the challenges posed by advancing AI technologies, particularly as they approach superintelligence.

item.title: sirius-chat 0.5.11
item.description: Sirius-chat 0.5.11 is a multi-user RPG dialogue framework that works with OpenAI providers, enhancing interactive storytelling in games.

item.title: What does history say about Islanders, Golden Knights late-season coaching change?
item.description: The Vegas Golden Knights and New York Islanders fired their head coaches with under 10 games left in the NHL season, raising questions about the timing and effectiveness of such drastic moves.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.28.0
item.description: Ouroboros-ai 0.28.0 also presents a specification-first workflow engine for AI coding agents, similar to the previous version.

item.title: Review: Apple’s new MacBook Air M5 is still the best laptop – but only just
item.description: Apple's new MacBook Air M5 offers doubled storage options but has removed the charger from the box, sparking mixed reviews on its value.

item.title: ouroboros-ai 0.27.3.dev8
item.description: Ouroboros-ai 0.27.3.dev8 continues the trend of specification-first workflow engines for AI coding agents, maintaining compatibility with Claude Code and Codex CLI.

item.title: azpaddypy 0.9.79
item.description: Azpaddypy 0.9.79 is an SDK for Azure cloud services, offering various features including storage solutions and AI project support.

item.title: Xiao-I Corp Stock Explodes 157% as China Court Victory Over Apple Patent Bolsters AI Patent Portfolio
item.description: Xiao-I Corp's stock surged 157% following a court victory over Apple, significantly boosting its AI patent portfolio and attracting retail investors.

item.title: stats-compass-mcp 0.3.8
item.description: The MCP server now provides access to stats-compass-core tools for large language models like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini. This integration enhances the capabilities of these LLMs by allowing them to utilize advanced statistical tools.

item.title: Big Tech Knows New AI Models Ripe For Cyberattacks — But Plans To Release Them Anyway
item.description: Tech companies are preparing to release new AI models that are particularly vulnerable to cyberattacks, raising concerns among AI and government officials. This decision could lead to significant security risks as these models can effectively hack complex systems.

item.title: simple-asr-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: A minimal MCP server for local speech recognition has been added to PyPI, utilizing the faster-whisper technology. This tool aims to improve local speech processing capabilities.

item.title: chalk-analytics added to PyPI
item.description: The chalk-analytics package has been introduced to PyPI, offering analytics for Claude Code usage. This tool aims to help developers understand how their code is being utilized.

item.title: XRP Open Interest Climbs As Traders Build Fresh Bearish Positions
item.description: XRP's open interest has increased, indicating that traders are opening new short positions as funding rates turn negative. This trend suggests a bearish sentiment among traders regarding XRP's future performance.

item.title: GoPro Shares New GP3 Teaser Ahead of Next-Gen Camera Launch
item.description: GoPro has released a teaser for its upcoming GP3 processor, hinting at advancements in its next-generation cameras. While specific products are not confirmed, the GP3 is expected to enhance camera performance significantly.

item.title: patchforge added to PyPI
item.description: The patchforge package has been added to PyPI, allowing users to run llama-server in the background and utilize Aider. This tool simplifies the process of managing server operations.

item.title: az-realtime-webrtc added to PyPI
item.description: The az-realtime-webrtc package has been released on PyPI, providing a Python SDK for the Azure OpenAI Realtime API, complete with WebSocket support for streaming and tool calling. This enhances real-time capabilities for developers.

item.title: jztree added to PyPI
item.description: The jztree package has been introduced to PyPI, offering a framework for Morton z-order trees optimized for JAX with a CUDA backend. This aims to improve computational efficiency in data processing.

item.title: jztree-cu13 1.0.0
item.description: The jztree-cu13 1.0.0 package has been released, providing a CUDA 13 backend for the jztree framework. This update aims to improve performance and compatibility with newer CUDA versions.
